summary: A biographical overview of Thomas Edison's career, detailing his transition from telegraph operator to full-time inventor. The text highlights his pioneering 'method of invention' through the creation of the Menlo Park research laboratory and his systemic approach to developing the electric lightbulb, phonograph, and telephone transmitter.

principles.statement: Combine existing fragmentary knowledge into a practical, operating whole.
principles.rationale: Successful innovation often requires building an entire supporting ecosystem (such as generators, distribution cables, and switches) rather than just a single isolated product (like a lightbulb).

frameworks.name: Industrial Research Laboratory
frameworks.when_to_apply: When pursuing continuous, large-scale technological innovation and basic research.
steps: Staff the laboratory with scientists engaged in basic research rather than immediate commercial products.
Work on overlapping projects simultaneously to allow discoveries in one area to inform another.
